heading: Administration of oaths and acknowledgments
label: 459 Administration of oaths and acknowledgments
text: Each justice or judge of the United States may administer oaths and affirmations and take acknowledgments.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 264, 385, , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Customs Duties, and , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Internal Revenue Code (, §§ 158, 268, , 1163; , title IV, § 509, ; , ; , title V, § 504(a), (c), ; , title V, § 503, ).section 1509 of title 19section 1114(a) of title 26Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 23136 Stat. 1139June 17, 1930, ch. 49746 Stat. 733Feb. 10, 1939, ch. 2, § 1114(a)53 Stat. 160Oct. 21, 1942, ch. 61956 Stat. 957Feb. 25, 1944, ch. 6358 Stat. 72Section consolidates provisions of sections 264 and 385 of title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., , U.S.C., 1940 ed., and , U.S.C., 1940 ed., relating to administration of oaths and acknowledgments by judges and courts.section 1509 of title 19section 1114(a) of title 26The provision of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., giving to “all courts of the United States” power to impose and administer all necessary oaths is the only part of such section in this title. The remainder is incorporated in section 401 of revised title 18, U.S.C. (H.R. 1600, 80th Cong.), Crimes and Criminal Procedure.section 385 of title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., related only to the Court of Claims and provision of such section relating to clerks and deputies is incorporated in .Section 264 of title 28section 953 of this title, U.S.C., 1940 ed., related only to the Customs Court.Section 1509 of title 19, U.S.C., 1940 ed., related only to The Tax Court. That portion of such section authorizing certain employees of The Tax Court to administer oaths and acknowledgments is incorporated in . For distribution of other provisions thereof, see Distribution Table.Section 1114(a) of title 26section 953 of this titleThe revised section clarifies what was apparently a statutory omission in that no provision was made with reference to the Court of Customs and Patent Appeals, the judges of which now will have the same power respecting administering oaths as judges of other courts.

Senate Revision AmendmentBy Senate amendment, all provisions relating to the Tax Court were eliminated, therefore, as finally enacted, , U.S.C., Internal Revenue Code, did not constitute part of the source of this section. However, no change in the text of this section was necessary. See 80th Congress Senate Report No. 1559.section 1114(a) of Title 26
